---
title: "Clinical Features"
author: "alexander-gysin (alexander.gysin@usz.ch)"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B, %Y')`"
output: workflowr::wflow_html
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: inline
---
```{r setup_boxes, include=FALSE}
source(here::here("code", "00_config.R"))
```

# Introduction
This script represents Phase 1b of the pipeline. It takes the technically synchronized data from Phase 1a and performs rigorous biological feature engineering, ordinal mapping, and statistical Quality Control (QA/QC). It addresses extreme skewness and class imbalance, generating a finalized curated_clinical_spine.rds and a clinical_data_dictionary.rds for all downstream omics scripts.

# Setup
`r what_this_does("Loaded required utility packages (tidyverse, data.table).", "Established dynamic output directory paths to safely save curated data.", "Loaded the raw_filtered_spine generated by Script 01a.")`

```{r setup, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Packages
library(tidyverse)
library(data.table)
library(here)
library(kableExtra)
library(patchwork)

# Knitr Config
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE
)

# Output paths
current_file <- "01b_clinical_feature_engineering"
output_dir_data <- here::here("output", current_file)
if (!dir.exists(output_dir_data)) dir.create(output_dir_data, recursive = TRUE)

# Load Technically Synced Data from 01a
raw_spine <- readRDS(here::here("output", "01a_data_import_and_qc", "raw_filtered_spine.rds"))
```

# Configuration
`r what_this_does("Established a global renaming dictionary for long clinical variables.", "Defined regex patterns to globally purge procedural meta-variables (e.g., 'measured?').", "Set strict mathematical thresholds for QA/QC, including a parameterized IQR multiplier for outlier detection.")`
```{r configuration}
# 0. Global File Paths ---------------------------------------------------------
file_paths <- list(
  clinical_dict_txt = "clinical_data_type_dictionary.txt" 
)

# 1. QC Mathematical Thresholds ------------------------------------------------
qc_config <- list(
  max_skewness = 2,        
  min_minority_pct = 0.10, 
  outlier_iqr_multiplier = 3.0, # <-- NEW: Parameterized multiplier for Tukey's Fences
  
  # Manual Overrides
  manual_drop_vars = c("...1" ),    
  manual_log_rejects = c(),  
  #log_Modified_rodnan_skin_score", "log_CRP", "log_Min_SpO2__Exercise", "log_Delta_PFT_Years", "10g_Delta_ECG_Years", "log_Delta_ECHO_Years"
  
  meta_drop_patterns = c("performed", "measured", "done", "velocity\\?", "ratio\\?", "area\\?", "excursion\\?", "fraction %\\?", "done\\?"),
  olink_date = as.Date("2026-03-30"),
  date_cols = c("Visit Date", "Entnahmedatum", "Einlagerungsdatum", 
                "If yes, Date of HRCT", "onset_nonRP", "Date of PFT", 
                "If yes, Date of assessment for ECG", "If yes, Date of assessment for ECHO",
                "If, yes Date of assessment for Walk test")
)

# 2. Variable Renaming Map -----------------------------------------------------
clinical_rename_map <- c(
  "Sclerodactyly" = "of the fingers as sclerodactyly (distal to the metacarpophalangeal joints but proximal to the proximal interphalangeal joints)",
  "SSc Antibody Score" = "Score: Scleroderma related antibodies (any of anti-centromere, Anti-topoisomerase I [ anti-SCL 70], anti-RNA polymerase III",
  "SSc Antibodies" = "Scleroderma related antibodies (any of anti-centromere, Anti-topoisomerase I [ anti-SCL 70], anti-RNA polymerase III",
  "Skin Thickening (Ext. MCP)" = "of the fingers of both hands extending proximally to the metacarpophalangeal joints",
  "Skin Thickening (Prox. MCP)" = "Skin thickening of the fingers of both hands extending proximal to the MCP joints",
  "New PAH" = "Does the patient have pulmonary arterial hypertension since the last visit?",
  "PAH/ILD" = "Pulmonary arterial hypertension and/or Interstitial lung Disease",
  "Max Borg Dyspnea" = "Worst modified Borg dyspnea score during the test (from 0 to 10)",
  "New Cardiac Manifest." = "Does the patient have cardiac manifestation since last visit?",
  "Diastolic Dysf. (Echo)" = "Diastolic function abnormal(on echo, E/a less than 10cm/sec)",
  "MD Global Activity (NA)" = "Physicians global assessment of disease activity VAS Unknown",
  "Pt Global Activity (NA)" = "Patients global assessment of disease activity VAS Unknown",
  "mRSS Worsening (1yr)" = "Was there a worsening of mRSS over the last 12 months?",
  "Intestinal Symptoms" = "Intestinal symptoms (diarrhea, bloating, constipation)",
  "Arthritis Activity (NA)" = "Activity of arthritis during the past week Unknown",
  "EULAR/ACR 2013 Criteria" = "2013 EULAR/ACR classification criteria fulfilled",
  "TAPSE (cm)" = "Tricuspid annular plane systolic excursion (cm)",
  "Stomach Symptoms" = "Stomach symptoms (early satiety, vomiting)",
  "FVC %pred" = "Forced Vital Capacity (FVC- % predicted)",
  "Capillaroscopy Score" = "Score: Abnormal nailfold capillaroscopy",
  "TLC %pred" = "Total lung capacity (TLC- % predicted)",
  "GAVE" = "Gastric Antral Vascular Ectasia (GAVE)",
  "LVEF %" = "Left ventricular ejection fraction %",
  "Min SpO2 (Exercise)" = "Worst O2-saturation at exercise (%)",
  "Capillaroscopy Pattern" = "Capillaroscopy scleroderma pattern",
  "Joint Contractures" = "Joint contractures (at any joint)",
  "Abnormal Capillaroscopy" = "Abnormal nailfold capillaroscopy",
  "Non-Raynaud Symptom" = "Any non-Raynaud symptom present?",
  "Puffy Fingers" = "of the fingers as puffy fingers"
)
```

# Feature Engineering
`r analysis_decision("Engineered cohort groups strictly from Subject_ID prefixes.", "Calculated standard baseline demographics (Age, Sex, Sample_Age) and temporal deltas between clinical assessments and blood draws.", "Permanently encoded Subsets (Diffuse/Limited/Sine) as One-Hot binary variables.", "Generated a Twin 'Numeric' Spine where severity rankings were mapped into mathematical ordinal scales (0-3) for downstream correlation engines.")`

`r what_this_does("Enforced the strict Schema Dictionary via external text file to coerce datatypes (n=numeric, c=factor) and drop flags (x).", "Renamed verbose columns via the mapping dictionary.", "Safely parsed raw Excel date serials into standard R Date objects.", "Applied One-Hot Encoding to subsets.", "Generated curated_spine_numeric by tracking Ordinal mappings and coercing factors to numerics, accompanied by a visual report.")`
```{r feature_engineering, results='asis'}
audit_log <- c("### Feature Engineering Log\n")
curated_spine <- copy(raw_spine)

# 0. Schema Enforcement (clinical_data_type_dictionary.txt) --------------------
regex_pattern <- paste(tolower(qc_config$meta_drop_patterns), collapse = "|")
meta_drops <- names(curated_spine)[str_detect(tolower(names(curated_spine)), regex_pattern)]
dict_drops <- c()

ext_dict_path <- here::here("data", file_paths$clinical_dict_txt)
if (file.exists(ext_dict_path)) {
  ext_dict <- fread(ext_dict_path)
  
  if(all(c("Variable_Name", "Type") %in% names(ext_dict))) {
    vars_drop <- ext_dict[tolower(Type) == "x"]$Variable_Name
    vars_num  <- ext_dict[tolower(Type) == "n"]$Variable_Name
    vars_cat  <- ext_dict[tolower(Type) == "c"]$Variable_Name
    
    dict_drops <- vars_drop
    
    # A. Execute Type Enforcement
    safe_numeric <- function(x) { suppressWarnings(as.numeric(gsub(",", ".", trimws(as.character(x))))) }

    num_cols <- intersect(vars_num, names(curated_spine))
    if (length(num_cols) > 0) curated_spine[, (num_cols) := lapply(.SD, safe_numeric), .SDcols = num_cols]

    # FIX: Categoricals must be FACTORS to keep them human-readable but grouped!
    cat_cols <- intersect(vars_cat, names(curated_spine))
    if (length(cat_cols) > 0) curated_spine[, (cat_cols) := lapply(.SD, as.factor), .SDcols = cat_cols]

    audit_log <- c(audit_log, "- Schema Enforcement: Forced explicit numeric (n) and factor (c) data types based on dictionary.")
  } else {
    audit_log <- c(audit_log, "- WARNING: Dictionary found but missing 'Variable_Name' or 'Type' columns.")
  }
} else {
  audit_log <- c(audit_log, "- WARNING: clinical_data_type_dictionary.txt not found.")
}

# Execute Drops
cols_to_drop <- unique(c(meta_drops, qc_config$manual_drop_vars, dict_drops))
valid_drops <- intersect(cols_to_drop, names(curated_spine))

meta_html <- ""
if (length(valid_drops) > 0) {
  curated_spine[, (valid_drops) := NULL]
  drop_reasons <- case_when(
    valid_drops %in% dict_drops ~ "Dictionary Flag ('x')",
    valid_drops %in% qc_config$manual_drop_vars ~ "Manual Config Override",
    TRUE ~ "Regex Meta-Variable"
  )
  
  meta_tbl <- data.frame(`Dropped Variable` = valid_drops, Reason = drop_reasons, check.names = FALSE) %>%
    arrange(Reason, `Dropped Variable`) %>%
    kableExtra::kbl(format = "html", row.names = FALSE, caption = "<b style='font-size:1.2em; color:#333;'>Dropped Clinical Variables</b>") %>%
    kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = TRUE, position = "left") %>%
    kableExtra::scroll_box(width = "100%", height = "300px")
  
  if (interactive()) print(meta_tbl) else meta_html <- as.character(meta_tbl)
  audit_log <- c(audit_log, sprintf("- Schema Enforcement: Dropped %d variables.", length(valid_drops)))
}

# 1. Feature Engineering (Groups, Dates, Age, Sex) -----------------------------
curated_spine[, cohort_group := fcase(
  str_detect(Subject_ID, "^Nonact"), GRP_NON_ACTIVE,
  str_detect(Subject_ID, "^Act"),    GRP_ACTIVE,
  str_detect(Subject_ID, "^Ctrl"),   GRP_CONTROL,
  default = NA_character_
)]
curated_spine[, cohort_group := factor(cohort_group, levels = c(GRP_ACTIVE, GRP_NON_ACTIVE, GRP_CONTROL))]

safe_excel_date <- function(x) { suppressWarnings(as.Date(as.numeric(as.character(x)), origin = "1899-12-30")) }
surviving_date_cols <- intersect(qc_config$date_cols, names(curated_spine))
if (length(surviving_date_cols) > 0) {
  curated_spine[, (surviving_date_cols) := lapply(.SD, safe_excel_date), .SDcols = surviving_date_cols]
}

curated_spine[, Entnahmedatum_Clean := Entnahmedatum]
curated_spine[, Birth_Year := as.numeric(trimws(Geburtsdatum))]
curated_spine[, Age := year(Entnahmedatum_Clean) - Birth_Year]
curated_spine[, Sample_Age := as.numeric(difftime(qc_config$olink_date, Entnahmedatum_Clean, units = "days")) / 365.25]

# FIX: Map Sex directly to the Global Config text strings and make it a factor!
curated_spine[, Sex := fcase(
  Geschlecht == "weiblich" | tolower(Geschlecht) == "female", SEX_FEMALE, 
  Geschlecht == "männlich" | tolower(Geschlecht) == "male", SEX_MALE,   
  default = NA_character_
)]
curated_spine[, Sex := factor(Sex, levels = c(SEX_MALE, SEX_FEMALE))]

# 2. Date Deltas ---------------------------------------------------------------
assessment_dates <- setdiff(surviving_date_cols, c("Visit Date", "Entnahmedatum", "Einlagerungsdatum"))
for (d_col in assessment_dates) {
  if (d_col %in% names(curated_spine)) {
    clean_prefix <- gsub(".*Date of assessment for |.*Date of ", "", d_col)
    clean_name <- paste0("Delta_", gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "_", clean_prefix), "_Years")
    curated_spine[, (clean_name) := as.numeric(difftime(get(d_col), Entnahmedatum_Clean, units = "days")) / 365.25]
  }
}

# 3. Variable Renaming & One-Hot Encoding --------------------------------------
setnames(curated_spine, old = as.character(clinical_rename_map), new = names(clinical_rename_map), skip_absent = TRUE)
setnames(curated_spine, old = c("OR serum creatinine (micromoles/L)", "CRP (mg/dl)", "Right atrium area (cm²)", "Right ventricular area (cm²)"), 
         new = c("Creatinine", "CRP", "Right atrium area (cm2)", "Right ventricular area (cm2)"), skip_absent = TRUE)

if ("Subset_diffuse" %in% names(curated_spine)) {
  curated_spine <- curated_spine %>%
    mutate(
      Subset_Diffuse = if_else(tolower(`Subset_diffuse`) == "diffuse cutaneous", 1, 0),
      Subset_Limited = if_else(tolower(`Subset_diffuse`) == "limited cutaneous", 1, 0),
      Subset_Sine    = if_else(tolower(`Subset_diffuse`) == "sine scleroderma", 1, 0)
    ) %>% select(-`Subset_diffuse`)
  audit_log <- c(audit_log, "- Applied One-Hot Encoding to SSc Disease Subsets.")
}

if("CRP" %in% names(curated_spine)) curated_spine[CRP <= 0, CRP := NA_real_]

# 4. Active Ordinal Mapping & Twin Spine Generation ----------------------------
# Clone the human-readable spine to create the math spine
curated_spine_numeric <- copy(curated_spine)

ordinal_map <- list(
  "Binary (no=0, yes=1)" = c("no" = "0", "yes" = "1"),
  "Temporal (never=0, prev=1, current=2)" = c("never" = "0", "previously" = "1", "current" = "2"),
  "Severity (none=0, rare=1, mod=2, ext=3)" = c("none" = "0", "rare" = "1", "moderate" = "2", "extensive" = "3"),
  "Roman Numerals (I=1, II=2, III=3)" = c("1" = "1", "i" = "1", "ii" = "2", "iii" = "3")
)

ordinal_log_df <- data.frame(Variable = character(), Mappings_Applied = character(), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
scan_cols <- names(curated_spine)[sapply(curated_spine, function(x) is.character(x) || is.factor(x))]

for (col in scan_cols) {
  vec <- tolower(as.character(curated_spine[[col]]))
  changed <- FALSE
  map_applied <- c()
  
  for (map_name in names(ordinal_map)) {
    mapping <- ordinal_map[[map_name]]
    if (any(vec %in% names(mapping), na.rm = TRUE)) {
      vec <- unname(mapping[vec]) %>% coalesce(vec)
      changed <- TRUE
      map_applied <- c(map_applied, map_name)
    }
  }
  
  if (changed) {
    # Apply changes strictly to the NUMERIC spine
    curated_spine_numeric[[col]] <- as.numeric(vec)
    ordinal_log_df <- rbind(ordinal_log_df, data.frame(Variable = col, Mappings_Applied = paste(map_applied, collapse = " | ")))
  } else if (col == "Sex") {
    # Explicit mapping for Sex into the math spine
    curated_spine_numeric[[col]] <- ifelse(curated_spine[[col]] == SEX_FEMALE, 1, 0)
    ordinal_log_df <- rbind(ordinal_log_df, data.frame(Variable = col, Mappings_Applied = "Explicit Binary (Male=0, Female=1)"))
  }
}

# Final Type Check for the Numeric Spine: Convert leftover text-numbers
curated_spine_numeric <- curated_spine_numeric %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ {
    if((is.character(.x) || is.factor(.x)) && all(grepl("^[0-9.]+$", na.omit(as.character(.x))))) {
      as.numeric(as.character(.x))
    } else {
      .x
    }
  }))
setDT(curated_spine_numeric)

audit_log <- c(audit_log, sprintf("- Generated curated_spine_numeric: mapped %d categorical variables to algebra-ready numerics.", nrow(ordinal_log_df)))

# 5. Output Render -------------------------------------------------------------
cat(paste(audit_log, collapse = "\n\n"))
cat("\n\n<br>\n\n")

if (meta_html != "") cat(meta_html)
cat("\n\n<br>\n\n")

if(nrow(ordinal_log_df) > 0) {
  ord_tbl <- ordinal_log_df %>%
    arrange(Mappings_Applied, Variable) %>%
    kableExtra::kbl(format = "html", row.names = FALSE, caption = "<b style='font-size:1.2em; color:#333;'>Ordinalization Tracker (Applied to Math Spine Only)</b>") %>%
    kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = TRUE, position = "left") %>%
    kableExtra::collapse_rows(columns = 2, valign = "top") %>%
    kableExtra::scroll_box(width = "100%", height = "400px")
  if (interactive()) print(ord_tbl) else cat(as.character(ord_tbl))
}
```

# Automated QC & Data Dictionary
`r analysis_decision("Continuous variables were evaluated for both skewness and extreme outliers simultaneously.", "Newly generated Log10 variables were forced through the same QC gauntlet to ensure the transformation successfully normalized the data and didn't mask unit-entry errors.", "Categorical variables with a minority class <10% were flagged as IMBALANCED.", "Character variables were deliberately bypassed from statistical QA/QC.")`

`r what_this_does("Iterated through every clinical feature routing by data type.", "Implemented an accumulating flag system (e.g., 'SKEWED & EXTREME OUTLIERS').", "Calculated Skewness and Outliers for both raw and newly transformed log variables, using is.finite() to protect against NaN/Inf crashes.", "Generated the definitive Data Dictionary object detailing the compound QC status.")`
```{r QC_and_Dictionary}
calc_skew <- function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  n <- length(x)
  if (n < 3) return(NA_real_)
  sum((x - mean(x))^3 / n) / (sd(x)^3)
}

calc_outliers <- function(x) {
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  if (length(x) < 5) return(0)
  q25 <- quantile(x, 0.25)
  q75 <- quantile(x, 0.75)
  iqr <- q75 - q25
  upper_bound <- q75 + (qc_config$outlier_iqr_multiplier * iqr)
  lower_bound <- q25 - (qc_config$outlier_iqr_multiplier * iqr)
  sum(x > upper_bound | x < lower_bound)
}

candidate_vars <- setdiff(names(curated_spine), c("Subject_ID", "inCentraxx", "Geburtsdatum", "Geschlecht", "Entnahmedatum", "Entnahmedatum_Clean", "Birth_Year", qc_config$date_cols))

dict_list <- list()
qc_plots_skew <- list()
imb_log_list <- list()

for (v in candidate_vars) {
  vec <- curated_spine[[v]]
  vec_patients <- vec[!is.na(curated_spine$cohort_group) & curated_spine$cohort_group != GRP_CONTROL]
  miss_pct <- sum(is.na(vec_patients)) / length(vec_patients)
  
  # A. CHARACTER / IDENTIFIER LOGIC
  if (is.character(vec)) {
    dict_list[[length(dict_list) + 1]] <- data.frame(
      Variable = v, Class = "Identifier / Text", Missing_Pct = miss_pct, 
      Distribution_Metric = "N/A (Free Text)", 
      QC_Flag = "PASS", Action_Taken = "Character bypassed QC"
    )
    next
    
  # B. NUMERIC / CONTINUOUS LOGIC
  } else if (is.numeric(vec)) {
    if (length(unique(na.omit(vec))) > 5) {
      skew_val <- calc_skew(vec_patients)
      extreme_outliers <- calc_outliers(vec_patients)
      
      # 1. Accumulate Flags for Raw Variable
      flags <- c()
      if (extreme_outliers > 0) flags <- c(flags, "EXTREME OUTLIERS")
      if (!is.na(skew_val) && abs(skew_val) > qc_config$max_skewness) flags <- c(flags, "SKEWED")
      
      qc_flag <- if (length(flags) > 0) paste(flags, collapse = " & ") else "PASS"
      dist_metric <- sprintf("Skew: %.2f | Outliers: %d", skew_val, extreme_outliers)
      action <- if ("SKEWED" %in% flags) "Log10 Transformed" else if ("EXTREME OUTLIERS" %in% flags) "Flagged (Review for unit errors)" else "None"
      
      # 2. Trigger Log Transformation if Skewed
      if ("SKEWED" %in% flags) {
        log_name <- gsub("_+$", "", paste0("log_", gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "_", v)))
        
        # Dynamically shift negative and zero variables to be strictly positive
        min_val <- min(vec_patients, na.rm = TRUE)
        if (is.finite(min_val) && min_val <= 0) {
          shift_val <- abs(min_val) + 1
          curated_spine[, (log_name) := log10(get(v) + shift_val)]
          curated_spine_numeric[, (log_name) := log10(get(v) + shift_val)]
        } else {
          curated_spine[, (log_name) := log10(get(v))]
          curated_spine_numeric[, (log_name) := log10(get(v))]
        }
        
        # 3. Test the NEW log variable
        log_patients <- curated_spine[[log_name]][!is.na(curated_spine$cohort_group) & curated_spine$cohort_group != GRP_CONTROL]
        log_skew <- calc_skew(log_patients)
        log_outliers <- calc_outliers(log_patients)
        
        log_flags <- c()
        if (log_name %in% qc_config$manual_log_rejects) {
          log_flags <- c(log_flags, "LOG REJECTED")
        } else {
          if (log_outliers > 0) log_flags <- c(log_flags, "EXTREME OUTLIERS")
          if (!is.na(log_skew) && abs(log_skew) > qc_config$max_skewness) log_flags <- c(log_flags, "SKEWED")
        }
        
        log_qc_flag <- if (length(log_flags) > 0) paste(log_flags, collapse = " & ") else "PASS"
        log_action  <- if ("LOG REJECTED" %in% log_flags) "Flagged by User" else "Engineered to fix skew"
        log_dist_metric <- sprintf("Skew: %.2f | Outliers: %d", log_skew, log_outliers)

        qc_plots_skew[[v]] <- local({
          plot_df <- data.frame(Raw = vec_patients, Transformed = log_patients)
          p_raw <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Raw)) + geom_histogram(fill = "#c0392b", color = "white", bins = 30) + theme_project_base(10) + labs(title = v, x = "Raw Data")
          p_log <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Transformed)) + geom_histogram(fill = "#2980b9", color = "white", bins = 30) + theme_project_base(10) + labs(title = log_name, x = "Log10 Data")
          p_raw | p_log
        })

        dict_list[[length(dict_list) + 1]] <- data.frame(Variable = log_name, Class = "Continuous", Missing_Pct = miss_pct, Distribution_Metric = log_dist_metric, QC_Flag = log_qc_flag, Action_Taken = log_action)
      }
      
      dict_list[[length(dict_list) + 1]] <- data.frame(Variable = v, Class = "Continuous", Missing_Pct = miss_pct, Distribution_Metric = dist_metric, QC_Flag = qc_flag, Action_Taken = action)
    } else {
      dict_list[[length(dict_list) + 1]] <- data.frame(Variable = v, Class = "Ordinal / Binary", Missing_Pct = miss_pct, Distribution_Metric = "N/A (<5 Levels)", QC_Flag = "PASS", Action_Taken = "None")
    }
    
  # C. FACTOR / CATEGORICAL LOGIC
  } else if (is.factor(vec) || is.logical(vec)) {
    props <- prop.table(table(vec_patients))
    counts <- table(vec_patients)
    min_prop <- if(length(props) > 0) min(props) else 0
    qc_flag <- "PASS"
    action <- "None"
    
    if (length(props) < 2) {
      qc_flag <- "ZERO VARIANCE"
      action <- "Flagged"
    } else if (min_prop < qc_config$min_minority_pct) {
      qc_flag <- "IMBALANCED"
      action <- "Flagged"
      imb_log_list[[v]] <- data.frame(Variable = v, Level = as.character(names(props)), Count = as.numeric(counts), Percentage = as.numeric(props))
    }
    
    dict_list[[length(dict_list) + 1]] <- data.frame(Variable = v, Class = "Categorical", Missing_Pct = miss_pct, Distribution_Metric = sprintf("Minority: %.1f%%", min_prop * 100), QC_Flag = qc_flag, Action_Taken = action)
  }
}

# Construct Dictionary
data_dictionary <- bind_rows(dict_list) %>%
  mutate(
    Missing_Pct = sprintf("%.1f%%", Missing_Pct * 100), 
    Sort_Order = case_when(
      grepl("ZERO VARIANCE", QC_Flag) ~ 1, 
      grepl("IMBALANCED", QC_Flag) ~ 2, 
      grepl("OUTLIERS", QC_Flag) ~ 3, 
      grepl("LOG REJECTED", QC_Flag) ~ 4, 
      grepl("SKEWED", QC_Flag) ~ 5, 
      QC_Flag == "PASS" ~ 6, 
      TRUE ~ 7
    )
  ) %>% 
  arrange(Sort_Order, Variable) %>% 
  select(-Sort_Order)

core_cols <- intersect(c("Subject_ID", "cohort_group", "Age", "Sex", "Sample_Age"), names(curated_spine))
setcolorder(curated_spine, core_cols)
setcolorder(curated_spine_numeric, core_cols)
```

## Data Dictionary
```{r data_dictionary, results='asis'}
# Print Interactive Table
dict_tbl <- data_dictionary %>%
  mutate(
    QC_Flag = case_when(
      QC_Flag == "PASS" ~ sprintf("<span style='color:#27ae60; font-weight:bold;'>%s</span>", QC_Flag),
      grepl("OUTLIERS|IMBALANCED|ZERO|REJECTED", QC_Flag) ~ sprintf("<span style='color:#c0392b; font-weight:bold;'>%s</span>", QC_Flag),
      grepl("SKEWED", QC_Flag) ~ sprintf("<span style='color:#2980b9; font-weight:bold;'>%s</span>", QC_Flag),
      TRUE ~ QC_Flag
    )
  ) %>%
  kableExtra::kbl(format = "html", escape = FALSE, row.names = FALSE) %>%
  kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = TRUE, position = "left") %>%
  kableExtra::scroll_box(width = "100%", height = "600px")

if (interactive()) print(dict_tbl) else cat(as.character(dict_tbl))
```

## QC Diagnostics {.tabset}

### Skewness Corrections
The following continuous variables violated the Skewness threshold (|Skew| > 2.5). The algorithm automatically engineered a normalized log_ variant.

```{r QC_diagnostics, results='asis'}
if (length(qc_plots_skew) > 0) {
  for (v in names(qc_plots_skew)) {
    cat(sprintf("#### %s\n\n", v))
    print(qc_plots_skew[[v]])
    cat("\n\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("> No severe skewness detected.\n\n")
}
```

### Extreme Outliers
The following continuous variables contain extreme outliers (values exceeding the parameterized IQR multiplier). These have been flagged for manual review to ensure they are not data entry or unit errors.

```{r qc_outliers, results='asis'}
# Query the dictionary for variables containing the outlier flag (handles compound flags)
outlier_vars <- data_dictionary %>% 
  filter(grepl("EXTREME OUTLIERS", QC_Flag)) %>% 
  pull(Variable)

if (length(outlier_vars) > 0) {
  for (v in outlier_vars) {
    cat(sprintf("#### %s\n\n", v))
    
    vec_patients <- curated_spine[[v]][!is.na(curated_spine$cohort_group) & curated_spine$cohort_group != GRP_CONTROL]
    
    if (sum(!is.na(vec_patients)) < 5) {
      cat("> Insufficient data to plot bounds.\n\n")
      next
    }
    
    plot_df <- data.frame(Value = vec_patients)
    
    q25 <- quantile(vec_patients, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
    q75 <- quantile(vec_patients, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
    iqr <- q75 - q25
    upper_bound <- q75 + (qc_config$outlier_iqr_multiplier * iqr)
    lower_bound <- q25 - (qc_config$outlier_iqr_multiplier * iqr)
    
    p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Value)) + 
      geom_histogram(fill = "#e67e22", color = "white", bins = 40) + 
      geom_vline(xintercept = c(lower_bound, upper_bound), color = "#c0392b", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
      theme_project_base(10) + 
      labs(title = v, 
           subtitle = sprintf("Red dashed lines indicate %gx IQR thresholds [%.1f, %.1f]", qc_config$outlier_iqr_multiplier, lower_bound, upper_bound), 
           x = "Data Value", y = "Count")
    
    print(p)
    cat("\n\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("> No extreme outliers detected.\n\n")
}
```

### Class Imbalances
The following categorical variables have a minority class of < 10%. They are retained in the dataset for Table 1 summaries, but flagged in the Dictionary to protect downstream omics models from zero-variance crashes.
```{r class_imbalances, results='asis'}
if (length(imb_log_list) > 0) {
  
  # Bind the list and calculate which specific rows triggered the flag
  imb_df <- bind_rows(imb_log_list) %>%
    mutate(
      Is_Offender = Percentage < qc_config$min_minority_pct,
      Percentage = sprintf("%.1f%%", Percentage * 100)
    ) %>%
    arrange(Variable, desc(Count)) # Sort levels by largest to smallest within each variable
  
  imb_tbl <- imb_df %>%
    select(Variable, Level, Count, Percentage) %>%
    kableExtra::kbl(format = "html", escape = FALSE, row.names = FALSE) %>%
    kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = TRUE, position = "left") %>%
    # Group the variable names so the table is extremely easy to read
    kableExtra::collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top") %>%
    # Highlight the offending minority classes in bold red!
    kableExtra::row_spec(which(imb_df$Is_Offender), bold = TRUE, color = "#c0392b") %>%
    kableExtra::scroll_box(width = "100%", height = "500px")
  
  if (interactive()) {
    print(imb_tbl)
  } else {
    cat(as.character(imb_tbl))
    cat("\n\n")
  }
  
} else {
  cat("> No severe class imbalances detected.\n\n")
}
```

# Clinical Domain Atlas {.tabset}
`r what_this_does("Iterates through the global clinical domain lists to construct a comprehensive visual atlas.", "Dynamically routes continuous variables to histograms (annotated with Median and IQR) and categorical variables to bar charts.", "Implements smart categorical sorting: recognized ordinal variables are plotted low-to-high, while nominal variables are sorted by frequency (Pareto principle).", "Bypasses the gitignored output folder by saving images directly to docs/assets/ and generating raw HTML links for web publishing.")`

```{r clinical_domain_atlas, results='asis'}
# Point directly to the web-accessible docs folder
atlas_dir <- here::here("docs", "assets", current_file)
if(!dir.exists(atlas_dir)) dir.create(atlas_dir, recursive = TRUE)

for (dom in names(clinical_domains)) {
  
  vars <- intersect(clinical_domains[[dom]], names(curated_spine))
  vars <- setdiff(vars, c("Subject_ID", "cohort_group"))
  if(length(vars) == 0) next
  
  plot_list <- list()
  
  for (v in vars) {
    vec <- curated_spine[[v]]
    vec_clean <- vec[!is.na(vec) & !is.na(curated_spine$cohort_group) & curated_spine$cohort_group != GRP_CONTROL]
    
    qc_row <- data_dictionary %>% filter(Variable == v)
    qc_flag <- if(nrow(qc_row) > 0) qc_row$QC_Flag[1] else "UNKNOWN"
    miss_pct <- if(nrow(qc_row) > 0) qc_row$Missing_Pct[1] else "UNKNOWN"
    
    flag_color <- case_when(
      qc_flag == "PASS" ~ "#27ae60",
      grepl("OUTLIERS|IMBALANCED|ZERO|REJECTED", qc_flag) ~ "#c0392b",
      grepl("SKEWED", qc_flag) ~ "#2980b9",
      TRUE ~ "black"
    )
    
    if (is.numeric(vec_clean)) {
      med <- median(vec_clean, na.rm=TRUE)
      q25 <- quantile(vec_clean, 0.25, na.rm=TRUE)
      q75 <- quantile(vec_clean, 0.75, na.rm=TRUE)
      sub_txt <- sprintf("QC: %s\nDist: Med %.1f [%.1f - %.1f]\nMissing: %s", qc_flag, med, q25, q75, miss_pct)
      
      p <- ggplot(data.frame(x=vec_clean), aes(x)) +
        geom_histogram(fill="grey70", color="white", bins=30) +
        geom_vline(xintercept=med, color="#2c3e50", linetype="dashed", linewidth=1) +
        theme_project_base(10) +
        labs(title = v, subtitle = sub_txt, x=NULL, y=NULL) +
        theme(plot.subtitle = element_text(color = flag_color, face="bold", size=rel(0.85)),
              plot.title = element_text(size=rel(0.9), face="bold"))
              
    } else {
      val_lower <- tolower(as.character(vec_clean))
      
      # SMART SORTING LOGIC: Ordinal vs Frequency
      if (all(val_lower %in% c("none", "rare", "moderate", "extensive"))) {
        plot_vec <- factor(val_lower, levels = c("none", "rare", "moderate", "extensive"))
      } else if (all(val_lower %in% c("never", "previously", "current"))) {
        plot_vec <- factor(val_lower, levels = c("never", "previously", "current"))
      } else if (all(val_lower %in% c("no", "yes"))) {
        plot_vec <- factor(val_lower, levels = c("no", "yes"))
      } else {
        # Nominal Data: Sort by Frequency (Pareto)
        plot_vec <- forcats::fct_infreq(as.character(vec_clean))
      }
      
      props <- prop.table(table(plot_vec)) * 100
      lvl_strs <- paste0(names(props), ": ", sprintf("%.0f%%", props))
      if(length(lvl_strs) > 3) lvl_strs <- c(lvl_strs[1:3], "...")
      
      sub_txt <- sprintf("QC: %s\nDist: %s\nMissing: %s", qc_flag, paste(lvl_strs, collapse=", "), miss_pct)
      
      p <- ggplot(data.frame(x=plot_vec), aes(x)) +
        geom_bar(fill="grey70", color="black", alpha=0.8) +
        theme_project_base(10) +
        labs(title = v, subtitle = sub_txt, x=NULL, y=NULL) +
        theme(plot.subtitle = element_text(color = flag_color, face="bold", size=rel(0.85)),
              plot.title = element_text(size=rel(0.9), face="bold"),
              axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1))
    }
    
    plot_list[[v]] <- p
  }
  
  grid_p <- wrap_plots(plot_list, ncol = 3) +
    plot_annotation(title = sprintf("Clinical Domain: %s", gsub("_", " ", dom)),
                    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size=18, face="bold")))
  
  dyn_h <- max(5, ceiling(length(plot_list) / 3) * 3 + 1)
  
  file_name <- paste0(dom, "_atlas.png")
  file_path <- file.path(atlas_dir, file_name)
  ggsave(file_path, grid_p, width=14, height=dyn_h, dpi=150, bg="white")
  
  cat(sprintf("## %s\n\n", gsub("_", " ", dom)))
  cat(sprintf("<img src='assets/%s/%s' style='width: 100%%;'>\n\n", current_file, file_name))
}
```

# Export
`r what_this_does("Exported the human-readable curated_clinical_spine.rds for visualization (Phase 2).", "Exported the binarized curated_clinical_spine_numeric.rds for machine learning and linear algebra (Phase 3).", "Exported the definitive clinical_data_dictionary.rds detailing the QC status.")`
```{r export}
saveRDS(curated_spine, file.path(output_dir_data, "curated_clinical_spine.rds"))
saveRDS(curated_spine_numeric, file.path(output_dir_data, "curated_clinical_spine_numeric.rds"))
saveRDS(data_dictionary, file.path(output_dir_data, "clinical_data_dictionary.rds"))

writexl::write_xlsx(curated_spine, file.path(output_dir_data, "curated_clinical_spine.xlsx"))

cat("Script 01b Complete!\n")
```
